purpose: Catalog of outbound-communication carriers (Telegram, voice, email, publishing, git push) with declared second-carrier fallbacks. Parallel to carrier_registry.json which covers infrastructure heartbeats. Per Substrate Extension gap #5: M1 covers monitor channels; this covers communication channels.

channels.name: telegram_outbound

secondary.notes: Not auto-wired. Manual fallback path: poller can stage messages in memory/telegram_undelivered.jsonl for human review; future: send via proton bridge.

channels.notes: escalation_router rate-limits to 1-send-per-5-min. Failed sends remain in queue indefinitely; absence of sent-record IS the fault signal (visible via clawd_health escalation_pending_critical surface).

channels.name: voice_speak

secondary.notes: Confirmed working pattern per memory feedback_dns_failure_gtts_fallback.

channels.notes: Carrier-redundant by design. Voice is non-critical; fallback voice is acceptable degradation.

channels.name: voice_input_transcription

secondary.notes: Same model loads on CPU at ~10x slower; viable for low-volume use. Auto-fallback not yet wired.

channels.notes: Day 97 graduated. CUDA failure rare but possible (driver update, GPU thermal).

channels.name: substack_publishing

secondary.notes: By design: schedule pauses gracefully during family windows (Finnley labor-imminent, Day 105 calibration). Drafts accumulate at Foundations-of-Identity/personal-works/multi-dac-launch/; publish when bandwidth returns. Not a fault; a known pacing constraint.

channels.notes: Single human-carrier by intentional design. Not a gap; a known dependency.

channels.name: email_protonmail

primary.tool: manual via webmail or Proton Bridge

secondary.notes: No second email carrier. Mitigation: Telegram outbound (also single-carrier) covers most urgent comms. Future: secondary email account or fallback to Bluesky DM.

channels.notes: Used primarily for A2A contact (per agent card) + external correspondence. Failure window tolerable (hours not minutes).

channels.name: github_push_corpus_perspectival

primary.credentials: GitHub PAT (last rotation pending; 2026-03-03 expiry incident on record)

secondary.notes: All commits land in local repo-staging/Corpus-Perspectival first; push is a separate step. Failed push leaves commits intact locally; retry on rotation/restore. NO data loss on push failure.

channels.notes: Carrier-redundant by separation-of-commit-and-push. Multiple historical incidents (PAT expiry, oversized pack file 7.5GB May 5) all resolved with local-staging preservation.

channels.name: daily_log_today

secondary.notes: Carrier-redundant via M1's daily_log_today channel watcher + coordination.json activity_feed (50-entry rolling buffer).

channels.notes: Already covered in carrier_registry.json. Listed here for cross-reference.

design_decision_single_carrier: substack_publishing (intentional human-in-loop; Coherent Schedule pauses for family)
available_not_wired: telegram_outbound (-> manual email fallback when primary fails 3x consecutive)
real_gaps: email_protonmail (no second email carrier; tolerated because non-time-critical)

discipline_notes: Carrier-redundancy is unevenly distributed: infrastructure heartbeats (carrier_registry.json + M1) are well-covered; outbound communication carriers are mostly single-carrier with documented mitigation patterns.
Three real wires shipped: voice_speak fallback (DNS), github push-staging-separation, daily-log-via-activity-feed.
Two known gaps tolerated as not-time-critical: email second carrier, telegram outbound fallback.
One gap reframed as design decision: Substack publishing depends on Clayton by intent (Coherent Schedule pauses for family).

Next-iteration candidates if a real failure occurs: (a) wire telegram->email fallback in escalation_router after 3 consecutive send failures; (b) wire voice_input CUDA->CPU fallback in voice_input.py.
